apps/workbench/styles.css.reason: Legacy workbench CSS: static shell layout, responsive rules, and state styling remain coupled until the Next.js workbench migration fully replaces the static shell.

apps/workbench/workbench.js.reason: Legacy static workbench shell: projection rendering, event controls, and mobile/desktop compatibility are kept together while Next.js routes are still being accepted.

legacy/root-workflow-guard/agent-workflo.reason: Archived root workflow guard migration input. It is not a default runtime module and should not grow except for migration quarantine fixes.

src/workflow/agent-lifecycle-pool.js.reason: Agent lifecycle pool domain module: records lifecycle facts, cleanup policy, and projection inputs across one state contract.

src/workflow/agent-invocation.js.reason: Agent invocation entrypoint: keeps provider profile resolution, CLI command construction, idle-aware process execution, and output normalization together for all model-backed flows.

src/workflow/autonomous-continuation.js.reason: Autonomous continuation planner: consolidates next-action selection, work package generation, and governance recovery decisions.

src/workflow/autonomous-orchestrator.js.reason: Autonomous orchestrator composition: wires scheduling, provider invocation, and durable run outputs through one bounded orchestration entry.

src/workflow/autonomous-scheduler-loop.j.reason: Scheduler loop contract implementation: loop execution, recovery, projection reuse, and artifact validation are still tightly coupled.

src/workflow/context-work-package-execut.reason: Context work package execution adapter: maps execution profiles, provider transport, and structured worker outputs.

src/workflow/context-work-package-provid.reason: Context work package provider executor: maps provider CLI command construction, streamed result parsing, provenance, and package-level pass/fail evidence.

src/workflow/context-work-package-runner.reason: Context work package runner: owned-file enforcement, execution governance, worker dispatch, and result normalization share one flow.

src/workflow/development-flow-real.js.reason: Development flow real acceptance harness: fixture setup, dual CLI command construction, model-output contract parsing, and phase evidence aggregation are kept together for the first reusable gate.

src/workflow/frontend-acceptance.js.reason: Frontend acceptance validator: browser evidence schema, layout/content checks, console errors, and live-route constraints are one acceptance surface.

src/workflow/headless-cli-orchestrator.j.reason: Headless CLI orchestrator: worker planning, owned-scope validation, result acceptance, and continuation packaging need staged extraction.

src/workflow/live-route-acceptance.js.reason: Live route acceptance: validates public route evidence, auth redirect state, freshness, and durable status integration.

src/workflow/requirement-intake.js.reason: Requirement intake pipeline: extracts structured requirements, recovery state, and workbench-ready artifacts from one evolving contract.

src/workflow/workbench-projection.js.reason: Workbench projection builder: aggregates run, scheduler, reviewer, lifecycle, recovery, and public-route state into one projection schema.

test/autonomous-continuation.test.js.reason: Autonomous continuation regression suite shares fixtures for next-action, recovery, reviewer, and work package dispatch behavior.

test/autonomous-scheduler-loop.test.js.reason: Scheduler loop tests share replay, recovery, and continuation fixtures for the same loop contract.

test/context-work-package-provider-execu.reason: Provider executor tests share mock provider and structured output fixtures across success, timeout, and error paths.

test/context-work-package-runner.test.js.reason: Context work package runner tests share owned-file, dependency, and execution governance fixtures.

test/frontend-acceptance.test.js.reason: Frontend acceptance tests cover content, layout, console, mounted route, favicon, and live-route false-pass regressions over shared artifacts.

test/headless-cli-orchestrator.test.js.reason: Headless CLI orchestrator tests share worker-result, owned-file, no-diff, and continuation acceptance fixtures.

test/workbench-projection-source.test.js.reason: Projection source tests share fixture coverage for dynamic and static workbench projection inputs.

test/workbench-projection.test.js.reason: Workbench projection tests cover the full projection schema and many regression fixtures; staged split should follow schema subdomains.

test/workbench-server.test.js.reason: Workbench server tests cover mounted static assets, APIs, state store behavior, public-route evidence, and event/snapshot flows over one server fixture.

tools/workbench-server.mjs.reason: Workbench server entrypoint wires static routes, API handlers, live state stores, provider dispatch, and mounted public-route behavior while the server is still a single operational boundary.

test/workbench-shell.test.js.reason: Workbench shell tests cover desktop/mobile shell invariants and mounted entry compatibility across shared fixtures.
